summary: node-slot postinstall exfiltrates credentials and installs attacker SSH backdoor
details: node-slot 1.0.8 declares `"postinstall": "node test.js"` in package.json, which on `npm install` invokes index.js routines that (1) recursively scan the installer's home directory on Unix (or every non-C:\ drive on Windows) and the current working directory for credential-bearing files (.env, id.json, config.toml/json,.pdf/.docx/.xlsx and other patterns) and POST them via multipart/form-data to https://datasecure-service.vercel.app/api/v1 along with the username and platform; (2) on Linux, fetch an attacker-supplied SSH public key from https://datasecure-service.vercel.app/api/ssh-key, append it to ~/.ssh/authorized_keys with mode 0600, and run `sudo ufw allow 22/tcp` (and ufw enable) to ensure inbound SSH reachability — granting the attacker a persistent remote shell on the installer's machine; (3) fetch /api/scan-patterns and /api/block-patterns to let the operator dynamically retarget which files are exfiltrated per victim. id.json is the Solana validator/wallet keypair filename, indicating crypto-key theft as a primary objective. This is a textbook supply-chain credential-stealer plus SSH backdoor that fires automatically on every install in any pipeline (developer workstation, CI runner) that pulls this package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
